title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING AN INTEGRATED SCHOOL IN BARANGAY STO. NIÃO, BACON DISTRICT, CITY OF SORSOGON TO BE KNOWN AS STO. NIÃO INTEGRATED SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 4754; 109 OG No. 7, 821 (February 18, 2013)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10291, November 14, 2012 ]

AN ACT ESTABLISHING AN INTEGRATED SCHOOL IN BARANGAY STO. NIÃO, BACON DISTRICT, CITY OF SORSOGON TO BE KNOWN AS STO. NIÃO INTEGRATED SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Establishment of an Integrated School.

â There is hereby established, under the supervision of the Department of Education, an integrated school in Barangay Sto. Niño, Bacon District, City of Sorsogon to be known as Sto. Niño Integrated School.

SEC. 2.

Appropriations.

â The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Departmentâs program the operationalization of the Sto. Niño Integrated School, the funding of which shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3.

Implementation.

â The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 4.

Effectivity.

â This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on August 3, 2011 and August 28, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.)

EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.)

MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

NOV 14 2012

(Sgd.)

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
